title: Recipes scaffold for opt-in domain-specific shapes
userStory: As a contributor, I want a clear extension point for crafting episodes from Notion content with domain knowledge, so that I can override the generic ingestion when my data has structure worth preserving (e.g., a tracker DB, a notes folder).
acceptanceCriteria: migrate/recipes/ exists with __init__.py and a README explaining the contract

Recipe contract: a callable recipe(item, context) -> PlannedEpisode | list[PlannedEpisode] | None, where item is the Notion row or page dict and context exposes notion_client + database_id/page_id + dry_run
CLI flag --recipe <module:function> overrides the generic shape; the migrator imports the dotted target and invokes it per item
If recipe returns None, fall back to the generic shape from US-008/US-009
No built-in personal recipes ship; only example shapes that demonstrate the contract (e.g., a 'plain page as one episode' recipe)
Verify: drop a sample recipe in recipes/, point --recipe at it, see the custom shape applied in --dry-run output
